How Much Does a Business Website Cost in Canada in 2026?
A practical guide for small business owners comparing simple websites, ecommerce websites, custom web applications, SEO, and long term website value.

Many Canadian business owners want a professional website, but they are not always sure what a fair price looks like. Some websites cost only a few hundred dollars, while others cost several thousand. The difference usually comes down to design quality, custom features, SEO setup, content, performance, security, and long term business value.
At Netvibes Infotech Ltd., we help businesses in Surrey, Vancouver, and across Canada build professional websites that are designed for trust, speed, search visibility, and lead generation.
What is a basic business website?
A basic business website is usually the best starting point for small service based companies that need a professional online presence. It normally includes a home page, about page, services page, contact page, basic SEO setup, mobile friendly design, and simple contact forms.
For many small businesses, this type of website is enough to build trust, explain services clearly, and make it easy for customers to get in touch.
For businesses that want a professional and affordable starting point, Netvibes Infotech offers a Business Website Starter Package designed for companies that need a clean, mobile friendly, and SEO ready website without unnecessary complexity.
What affects the cost of a website?
Website cost depends on several things. The biggest factors are the number of pages, design quality, custom features, content writing, image optimization, SEO setup, forms, analytics, speed optimization, and whether the website needs ecommerce or custom software features.
A simple five page business website costs less than a full ecommerce store or a custom dashboard system. The more business logic and automation a website needs, the more planning and development work is required.
How much does an ecommerce website cost?
An ecommerce website costs more because it needs product pages, cart, checkout, payment setup, shipping settings, order management, customer emails, security, and product SEO.
A proper ecommerce website should not only look good. It should make buying simple for customers and make order management easy for the business owner.
What is a custom web application?
A custom web application is usually built when a business needs more than a regular website. This may include customer dashboards, booking systems, invoice systems, internal tools, automation, reporting, or custom admin panels.
For these projects, Django and Python are strong choices because they are flexible, secure, and scalable.
Why cheap websites can become expensive
A cheap website may look fine at the beginning, but it can create problems later if it is slow, hard to update, not mobile friendly, poorly structured for SEO, or difficult to expand.
Business owners should look at long term value, not just the first price. A professional website should support marketing, sales, trust, and future growth.
What should be included in a professional website?
A professional website should include a clean design, mobile responsive layout, fast loading speed, SEO friendly structure, proper heading tags, optimized images, contact forms, analytics setup, security, and clear calls to action.
It should also be easy for visitors to understand what the business offers and how to contact the company.
Final advice
Before choosing a website package, business owners should ask what is included, how SEO is handled, who owns the website, whether the site can be updated later, and whether the developer understands business goals.
